name: Jannah
description: Jannah, meaning 'garden' or 'paradise' in Arabic, is the ultimate reward promised to righteous believers in Islam. It is a realm of unimaginable beauty, eternal bliss, and perfect peace, described in vivid detail throughout the Quran and Hadith. Jannah is not a single place but consists of multiple levels or gardens, each surpassing the previous in splendor and reserved for different levels of piety and devotion. Rivers of pure water, milk, honey, and wine flow freely, nourishing the inhabitants with delightful sustenance. Mansions of pearl, ruby, and emerald await the believers, furnished with luxurious carpets and adorned with precious gems. The dwellers of Jannah are eternally young and beautiful, free from all sorrow, pain, and suffering. They are granted perfect health and companionship, enjoying the presence of their loved ones and the angels. The ultimate reward in Jannah is the beatific vision of Allah (God), the greatest joy and fulfillment imaginable. This blessed abode represents the culmination of a life lived in obedience to Allah and a testament to His boundless mercy and grace. It is a place where all desires are fulfilled, and all needs are met, a true reflection of divine perfection and eternal happiness. Access to Jannah is earned through faith, good deeds, repentance, and the mercy of Allah.
subtitle: The Garden of Eternal Bliss

keyMyths.title: Descriptions of Jannah in the Quran
keyMyths.description: Various verses in the Quran describe Jannah with lush gardens, flowing rivers, and beautiful companions, promising eternal bliss to believers. These descriptions serve as motivation for righteous living.

keyMyths.title: Levels of Jannah
keyMyths.description: Jannah has multiple levels, each with varying degrees of reward and reserved for different levels of piety. The highest level, Al-Firdaus, is closest to Allah's presence.

sources.author: Various Translators
sources.description: The primary source for descriptions of Jannah.
sources.text: The primary source for descriptions of Jannah.

sources.author: Various Compilers
sources.work: Hadith Collections (e.g., Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim)
sources.description: Collections of sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad providing further details about Jannah.
